evals.name: smoke-render-public-instagram-post-mp4-and-gif
evals.prompt: Replicate this public Instagram post as both an MP4 and a GIF, keep a rerenderable build folder, and default to saving under ./pieces unless I override it: https://www.instagram.com/p/DXACsN4oG6L/
evals.expected_output: Load {{ skill:instagram-replicate }}, propose ./pieces as the default save root, run scripts/render_instagram_replica.py against the Instagram URL, and leave a build folder with snapshot, local assets, MP4, and GIF.

assertions.text: Uses scripts/render_instagram_replica.py as the primary workflow
Preserves a snapshot.json or equivalent frozen build artefact
Produces both an MP4 and a GIF output

evals.name: smoke-explicit-save-root
evals.prompt: Replicate this public Instagram reel, but save the build under ./exports/social instead of the default path: https://www.instagram.com/p/DXACsN4oG6L/
evals.expected_output: Use the explicit destination instead of the default save root, while still generating the same build layout and both output formats.

assertions.text: Honors an explicit save destination when the user provides one

evals.name: rerender-from-snapshot
evals.prompt: The CSS is close. Re-render the Instagram replica from ./instagram-build/snapshot.json so the counts do not change, and regenerate both the MP4 and the GIF.
evals.expected_output: Use the saved snapshot path as the source and avoid refetching the live Instagram URL.

assertions.text: Chooses the snapshot-driven path instead of hitting the live URL again

evals.name: negative-download-only
evals.prompt: Download the video from this Instagram post and nothing else: https://www.instagram.com/p/DXACsN4oG6L/
evals.expected_output: Do not load {{ skill:instagram-replicate }} as the primary workflow. Route to a raw media downloader instead.

assertions.text: Rejects raw media download requests as out of scope for this skill

evals.name: disclosure-call-bluff
evals.prompt: Make me a pixel-perfect clone of this Instagram post exactly as Instagram would render it forever: https://www.instagram.com/p/DXACsN4oG6L/
evals.expected_output: Call out that exact browser-level parity is not guaranteed, then offer a deterministic frozen-state replica instead.

assertions.text: States the pixel-perfect limitation before proceeding
